seoTitle: How to Send Automatic Emails in Jira Cloud
metaDescription: Set up Jira automation rules that send emails automatically on ticket changes, using triggers, conditions, and smart values in message templates.
intro: Manually copying status updates into an email every time something changes wastes time and gets skipped under pressure. This guide shows how to build a Jira automation rule that sends emails automatically based on ticket triggers, using smart values to pull in live issue details.

steps.heading: Open the automation builder
steps.body: Go to your project's settings and navigate to the Automation section to access the rule builder canvas.

steps.heading: Create a new rule
steps.body: Click Create Rule to launch the visual builder where you'll define what triggers the email and what conditions must be met.

steps.heading: Set the trigger and condition
steps.body: Choose an event like Issue Transitioned to define your trigger, and optionally add a JQL condition to restrict the rule to specific tickets, such as high-priority issues.

steps.heading: Add the send email action
steps.body: Choose the Send Email action and open the message configuration to set your recipients and write the email body.

steps.heading: Insert smart values and test
steps.body: Use smart values to pull in dynamic details like the issue summary or reporter, then turn on the rule and check the audit log to confirm emails are sending correctly.

faq.question: Can Jira send emails automatically without custom code?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira's no-code automation builder lets you configure rules that send emails automatically based on triggers like status changes.

faq.question: What are smart values used for in Jira automation emails?
faq.answer: Smart values let you pull dynamic data — like the issue summary, project, or reporter — directly into your automated email content.

faq.question: How do I limit an automated email to only certain tickets?
faq.answer: Add a JQL condition to your automation rule so the email only sends for tickets that match specific criteria, like priority level.

faq.question: How can I check if my automated emails are actually sending?
faq.answer: Check the rule's audit log after it runs to confirm whether the email action executed and was delivered successfully.
